Biological_Mechanism: Hindgut secretion; deposited via abdomen touching substrate
Behavioral_Response: Recruitment and guidance to food; facilitates route learning and results in faster, straighter walking speeds
Decay_Rate_or_Persistence: Ephemeral; build-up is suppressed by negative feedback from existing high pheromone levels
Foraging_Context: Sucrose foraging on doubly bifurcating mazes (alternating vs. repeating routes)

Biological_Mechanism: Passively deposited from tarsi while walking
Behavioral_Response: Repellent effect; workers exhibit negative chemotaxis by preferentially choosing branches with lower concentrations
Decay_Rate_or_Persistence: Low volatility; stable over hours or days; long-lasting persistence
Foraging_Context: Foraging in environments with competing trail branches; aids in abandoning unrewarding trails

Biological_Mechanism: Active deposition on unrewarding trail branches after bifurcation
Behavioral_Response: Avoidance of unrewarding areas; improves colony efficiency in switching to new food sources
Decay_Rate_or_Persistence: Effective for approximately 33 minutes; persists longer than the species' attractive pheromones
Foraging_Context: Searching for food and optimizing search tactics

Biological_Mechanism: Sting dragging; deposited as discontinuous spots or continuous lines
Behavioral_Response: Mass recruitment to food sources; provides guidance and stimulation for workers to leave the nest
Decay_Rate_or_Persistence: Includes both long-lasting and short-lasting variants
Foraging_Context: Opportunistic scavenging and rapid exploitation of transient food sources

Behavioral_Response: Increased running speed; positive chemotaxis (aggressive alarm response); gaster vibrations
Decay_Rate_or_Persistence: Calculated lifetime of approximately 27.5 minutes (based on  L. claviger  data)
Foraging_Context: Possible recruitment to unexplored territory during mass exploration phases

Biological_Mechanism: Correlated with maturity and fecundity; abundant on queen-laid eggs
Behavioral_Response: Inhibits worker ovarian development; reduces worker aggression toward the queen
Decay_Rate_or_Persistence: Signal strength is depressed by immune challenges (health-dependent signal)
Foraging_Context: Regulation of reproductive division of labor and worker sterility

Biological_Mechanism: Trophallaxis (mouth-to-mouth transfer); storage in the 'social stomach'
Behavioral_Response: Negative feedback: existing food volume within colony inhibits entry of more food; loaded ants remain in center of aggregate
Foraging_Context: Feeding and management of food stocks to prevent shortages
